name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value greater than the original input

name: align_result_not_multiple
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_result_not_multiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The result of downward alignment must be a multiple of the alignment boundary

name: aligned_idempotent_fails
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_idempotent_fails(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: If the input is already aligned, align_to_usize must be the identity—returning a different value is a spec inconsistency

name: zero_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_4_zero_not_aligned(size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(0, size),
{
}
reason: Zero is trivially divisible by any positive number, so aligned(0, size) must be true

name: align_noop_when_unaligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_noop_when_unaligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b != 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == a,
{
}
reason: When the input is not aligned, align_to_usize must adjust it downward—returning it unchanged means alignment is broken
